caption: Initial-frame Faraday SNR as a function of F=P tau at |Delta|/2pi=1.5 GHz for the undepleted reference condensate. The estimator uses the absolute expected signal in the central 5x5 camera-pixel block and includes Poisson noise plus 0.7 e- rms read noise per pixel and port. The dashed line marks the working SNR=8 camera-space screening threshold. The labelled conditions are A (F=90 mW us), B (F=150 mW us), C (F=300 mW us). The lower panels are fixed noise realisations and do not determine the curves.
faraday_boundary: ideal atomic coefficient fixed at -45/91; apparatus-level response remains to be calibrated

fixed_context.central_block_basis: fixed local estimator spanning 3.25 um, compared with the 1.88 um ideal Rayleigh scale at effective NA=0.130
fixed_context.faraday_calibration_status: ideal atomic coefficient fixed at -45/91; apparatus-level response remains to be calibrated
fixed_context.quantum_efficiency_status: catalogue typical at 400 nm; provisional at 401 nm until measured in the installed path
fixed_context.read_noise_status: manufacturer-typical rms value for Ultra quiet scan; provisional until verified for the installed camera, ROI and trigger timing

label: figure_5_1_faraday_snr_and_camera_frames_orca_fusion_m10_v4_na013
noise_draw_boundary: one fixed representative draw per mode and selected fluence; random draws illustrate camera appearance and do not estimate the analytic SNR

scope.camera_noise: Poisson variance plus the ORCA-Fusion Ultra quiet manufacturer-typical value sigma_r=0.7 e- rms per physical pixel and readout
scope.prediction_status: screening calculation within the Version 1 forward model, not guaranteed experimental performance
scope.state: same undepleted reference condensate at every fluence

screening_guide.interpretation: working camera-space screening threshold for the spatial-structure task; not a universal visibility boundary

selected_frames.interpretation: fixed representative draws for camera appearance; not used to estimate SNR
